title: Role-Based Permissions and User Onboarding Experience
story: As a Salesforce administrator managing a DataTools Pro team, I want to assign predefined permission levels (Read Only, Editor, Admin) to invited users and provide them a welcoming onboarding experience, so that I can control access appropriately and new users understand what DataTools Pro offers.
description:
Implement a simplified permission system with three predefined roles and improve the user invitation/onboarding experience.

## Permission Roles

1. **Read Only** — View-only access, no editing capability.
2. **Editor** — Can edit objects and content. Sharing permission can be granted on top.
3. **Admin** — Full control including invite and manage users.

Permissions are organized by module (Dictionary, Migration, Metrics, ERD, etc.) with checkboxes. Role shortcuts (Read Only, Editor, Admin) auto-select the appropriate permission checkboxes. A Reset button deselects all permissions.

## Invitation Email Updates

- Email uses the Salesforce email of the inviter (not the beta signup email).
- Indicates whether the account is Production or Sandbox.
- Optional text box for the admin to add notes to the invitation.
- Email is sent only to the invited user, not the inviter.

## Login Page Updates for Invited Users

Welcome message: "Welcome to DataTools Pro! Thank you for accepting your invite..." with context about the application capabilities. Invitation link includes the passcode, bypassing the passcode entry screen. After Salesforce authentication, users are automatically redirected to accept the invitation.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can assign Read Only, Editor, or Admin roles via checkbox shortcuts
Selecting a role auto-checks the appropriate permission checkboxes
Permissions are organized by module (Dictionary, Migration, Metrics, ERD, etc.)
A Reset button deselects all permissions
Invitation email uses the inviter's Salesforce email address
Email indicates Production or Sandbox environment
Only the invited user receives the invitation email
Login page shows a welcome message for invited users
Invitation link includes passcode to bypass passcode screen
After authentication, invited users are redirected to accept the invitation
Read Only role checkbox appears at the top of the permission list
ux_changes: Permission management UI organized by module with role shortcut checkboxes (Read Only, Editor, Admin, Reset). Updated login page with welcome message for invited users. Invitation flow bypasses passcode screen using embedded passcode in the link.
middleware_changes: Permission model updated with module-based grouping. Role shortcut logic auto-selects appropriate permissions. Invitation acceptance flow handles passcode embedding and post-auth redirection.
data_model_changes: Permission model restructured to support module-based grouping. Role shortcuts mapped to specific permission sets.
api_changes: Permission update endpoint modified to use role-based permission assignment. Invitation acceptance flow updated.
implementation_notes: Developer initially proposed a full role-based access control (RBAC) system with custom roles, but product owner opted for the simpler three-tier approach (Read Only/Editor/Admin) to avoid complexity. Permissions are grouped by module in the UI. The invitation link embeds the passcode and redirects to the invitation acceptance page after Salesforce authentication. Environment selection (Production/Sandbox) is displayed in the team member list. A typo in 'Dictionary' was caught and fixed during review.
comment_summary: Developer proposed full RBAC with custom roles but product owner simplified to three preset roles to avoid unnecessary complexity. Permissions were reorganized by module. The invitation flow was enhanced to embed the passcode in the link, bypass the passcode screen, and auto-redirect after authentication. Product owner requested Read Only role at top, fixed a Dictionary typo, and ensured Admin selects all checkboxes.
